obligation_text: Providers of high-risk AI systems shall put a quality management system in place that ensures compliance with this Regulation. That system shall be documented in a systematic and orderly manner in the form of written policies, procedures and instructions, and shall include at a minimum: a strategy for regulatory compliance; techniques, procedures and systematic actions to be used for the design, design control and design verification of the high-risk AI system; techniques, procedures and systematic actions to be used for the development, quality control and quality assurance of the high-risk AI system; examination, test and validation procedures to be carried out before, during and after the development of the high-risk AI system; technical specifications; and systems and procedures for data management.
scoring_text: Skill in designing and implementing AI quality management systems, including regulatory compliance strategy, design control and verification procedures, development quality control, examination and validation procedures throughout the AI lifecycle, technical specification management, and data management systems.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Skill in designing and implementing AI quality management systems, including regulatory compliance strategy, design control and verification procedures, development quality control, examination and validation procedures throughout the AI lifecycle, technical specification management, and data management systems.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in conducting AI conformity assessments under the EU AI Act, including risk classification determination, technical documentation evaluation, and compliance assessment against applicable high-risk AI system requirements.
comments: EU AI Act Art. 17(1) (obligation) superset of WIDAI RC domain. RC-S-008 is the strongest match (STS: 0.612, cosine: 0.694). Secondary: SP-T-010 (SP, cosine: 0.694), AG-K-004 (AG, cosine: 0.681).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the regulatory requirement) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.1/10 (raw 0.612). Shared vocabulary: [ai, compliance, skill, technical]. EU AI Act competency implication terms [control, data, design, designing, development, examination] vs WIDAI terms [act, against, applicable, assessment, assessments, classification] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.886 (P=0.876, R=0.896). Balanced P/R — the 36-word competency implication and 27-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs (mean ~0.007) because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (54.9%). Semantic tension between "Skill in designing and implementing AI quality management systems, including reg" and "Skill in conducting AI conformity assessments under the EU AI Act, including ris". Despite STS-based 'Superset of' classification, these concepts may operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.694 > STS 0.612 (gap +0.082). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [ai, compliance, skill, technical] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 18.6%. Shared tokens: [ai, compliance, skill, technical]. EU AI Act-only: [control, data, design, designing, development, examination]. WIDAI-only: [act, against, applicable, assessment, assessments, classification].

signals.detail: NLI contradiction 54.9% suggests semantic tension between the FDE and matched KSA. The competency domains may be adjacent but not functionally aligned.
